strengths: Direct match on primary stack: TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS, and MongoDB all appear across multiple roles with production-scale usage
Active AI agent and MCP infrastructure experience at current role (re:cinq), directly satisfying the AI agent development requirement
Deep testing culture evidenced by TDD practice, published D-language testing libraries, and organized testing workshops — aligns well with Vitest/Jest expectations
concerns: No mention of NestJS anywhere in the resume; the primary backend framework for this role is absent
Kotlin/Java exposure is limited to embedded/robotics context (2016–2018) and not recent enterprise backend work
Relational database experience (PostgreSQL, MySQL) is sparse; most recent database work centers on MongoDB and niche high-performance stores
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong match on the fundamentals — TypeScript, Node.js, React, cloud tooling, and testing discipline are all well-evidenced across a 15-year career that spans production-scale systems. The AI agent and MCP work at re:cinq is a direct hit on an explicit requirement that many candidates will lack. The main gap is NestJS: it is the stated primary backend framework and does not appear anywhere in the resume, which is a meaningful unknown for a mid-senior IC role expected to own backend architecture. Kotlin/Java exposure exists but is dated and low-stakes, which could be a gap for the secondary backend services. Overall the candidate is technically mature with strong fundamentals and a rare AI tooling background; the NestJS question is the key item to probe in a screen.
